title: platanthera bifolia var. trifoliata
intro: Platanthera bifolia var. trifoliata, also known as the lesser butterfly orchid, is a subspecies of terrestrial orchid native to the mountainous regions of Europe. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The lesser butterfly orchid has been used to treat various health issues, including coughs, bronchitis, and respiratory problems, as well as to aid in digestion and alleviate menstrual cramps. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal benefits and promote overall well-being.
